info.title: Sovereign Hybrid Compute User API
info.description:
Phase 4 adds hygiene artifacts: seal-master rotation runbook, PGP weak-key warning, prove-recovery telemetry shape, and downgraded his-spec RCCPP gates. His-spec C.3/C.4 claims remain warning-level until production constant-time ML-KEM and real two-server XOR-PIR are deployed.

Phase 3 adds both-leg recipient binding for pq-hybrid recipients: pubkey_x25519_hex, pubkey_mlkem_hex, and reader_id=SHA256(x25519_pub||mlkem_pub) are folded into recipientSetHash and active seal digest. It also publishes derivation and KAT contract artifacts and documents the PQ seal AAD transcript binding.

Phase 2 adds an explicit coherent ZK backup recipient API: register, status, full recipient list, revoke, and retention-aware rekey. SHC ZK backup is genuine self-custody, the same model as Bitcoin: your keys, your data. Backups already sealed to a recovery key stay openable by that key until you rotate forward and re-upload the backups you care about; that is the sovereignty property. If a recovery key is exposed, register a fresh recipient set and re-upload replacement backups, like sweeping a Bitcoin key to a fresh address. SHC cannot re-seal, claw back, or reach into existing backup data; that inability is the guarantee. Operator sign-off is required before deploying this self-custody rotation design fork.

v2.4.14 is an intentional security hardening release: getVirtualMachineCredentials is again classified as x-shc-secret-read and x-shc-confirm-required, reversing the 2026-06-24 un-gate because it returns a reusable decrypted VM password. First ZK backup registration now requires X-User-Api-Confirm before the initial customer recipient set is written; ZK rekey is confirm-gated rotate-forward self-custody with chosen-rung retention and preserved historical openability. Flag this release note for Amperstrand contract consumers.

v2.4.11 adds the Blesta-native switchManagedAccount operation for same-company managed-account acting-as, with dual-identity audit and confirm-gated activation.

v2.4.9 adds native batch submission: POST /batch accepts up to 25 live /user-api/v2 sub-requests, preserves response order and correlation ids, reports partial failures per item, requires batch-level idempotency, rejects nested batch recursion, and dispatches each sub-request through the same native guard, confirmation, ownership, rate, and idempotency chain as a standalone call. A batch consumes one rate unit per sub-request and does not roll back successful siblings.

v2.4.8 adds native event webhook push subscriptions: create, list, get, and delete customer-owned HTTPS subscriptions for the existing CloudEvents feed. Delivery is asynchronous, signed with HMAC-SHA256, re-validates public HTTPS egress at delivery time, retries with bounded backoff, and records exhausted attempts in a dead-letter store. The signing secret is returned once on create and is never returned by list, get, delete, or idempotent replay.

Customer-facing VM API for authenticated SHC Blesta customers. Use it to read account data, compare storefront-backed VM plans, preview and submit a new VM order, inspect owned VM inventory, manage power, reinstall, backup, snapshot, and restore workflows, review invoices, and manage stored SSH keys. `GET /ordering/catalog` is the primary authenticated buy catalog, `POST /ordering/preview` validates a request, and `POST /ordering/submit` or `POST /vm/buy` creates the order through Blesta's canonical order engine. Backup, snapshot, and restore routes queue background jobs, and `GET /vm/{serviceId}/jobs` or `GET /vm/{serviceId}/jobs/{jobId}` exposes job progress. `GET /vm/templates` remains a descriptive plan and image endpoint for the customer's eligible VM scope and owned-service workflows. The `/user-api/v2` REST is a deprecated legacy transport, kept for backward compatibility; the flagship MCP (see the MCP note below) is the supported, go-forward agent surface. The wrapper handles authentication, rate limiting, routing, and response formatting, while an internal Blesta plugin model backs the business logic. Direct native `/api/UserApi.UserApiApi/...` access is intentionally disabled for customer-scoped methods. HTTP Basic uses your Blesta login username and password. For many accounts the username is the email address, but that is not guaranteed for every account type. If the account has 2FA enabled, send the current code in the `X-User-Api-OTP` header. Missing credentials return `401 Authentication required`. Missing OTP returns `401 Two-factor code required` and sets `X-User-Api-OTP-Required: 1`. Invalid password and invalid OTP also return `401` with endpoint-specific error messages. JSON request bodies are capped at 16 KiB. VM IDs in this API are Blesta service IDs, not Proxmox VMIDs. MCP note: this API exposes account, catalog, order, invoice, service, storage, and job state. It does not provide ambient screen or browser context by itself. The eligible authenticated operations (169 MCP tools with X-MCP-Tools: all; the default tools/list returns 30 curated tools; binary-response operations are REST-only) are exposed as MCP tools at the flagship server https://mcp.sovereignhybridcompute.com/ (Model Context Protocol over Streamable HTTP), the preferred agent transport, with single-use confirmation_id gating on every spend and destructive operation; authenticate with your shc_live_ operate key as a Bearer token. v1.8.0 documents the previously-undocumented Tier-2 write endpoints: VM shutdown/reset, ISO mount/unmount, firewall rule/policy management, live SSH key inject/remove, backup/snapshot verify acknowledgement, data-preferences update, support ticket create/reply/close, account preferences and credit-handling updates, contact deletion, and manager / managed-account management. Reinstall now returns 202 with a pollable job_id. v1.10.0 adds the Tier-2 service upgrade / package-change endpoints: GET /vm/{serviceId}/upgrade-options (read-only catalog of same-group plans + settable config options), POST /vm/{serviceId}/upgrade/preview (read-only prorated quote), and PATCH /vm/{serviceId}/upgrade (the money-moving change). The PATCH is QUEUED-ONLY: it always creates a prorated invoice and a queued change and returns 202; the VM is resized by Blesta's standard cron after the invoice is paid. pricing_ref is the raw package_pricing id. A disk-reducing change is always rejected (422) because the module would destroy and recreate the VM. The PATCH requires a body idempotency_key. v1.12.0 documents two shipped features: native AREA-scoped customer API keys and the enriched account activity report. Customer API keys (`Authorization: Bearer shc_live_...`, created/listed/revoked at /account/api-keys with Basic+OTP) now carry TWO ANDed authorization axes - a METHOD scope (read / operate / full) and an OPTIONAL area subset of Blesta's native contact-permission areas; each operation declares its governing area via `x-required-area`, a key created with no `areas` is materialized to the account's full permitted-area set (never stored unrestricted), and a key created with an `areas` array may only reach operations whose area is in the set (see the customerApiKey security scheme). GET /account/activity now merges sign-ins, failed sign-ins, account contact-detail changes, account-setting changes, and payment/transaction activity into one most-recent-first paginated feed (ActivityItem); it never exposes internal placement, staff identity, or the old/new values of changed fields. v1.14.0 expands the orderable OS/desktop catalog: Linux templates devuan5-cloud (Devuan 5) and openbsd79-cloud (OpenBSD 7.9), desktop environments kde (KDE Plasma) and mate (MATE) for AlmaLinux 9, and windows2022-byol (Windows Server 2022, Bring Your Own License). windows2022-byol is a dedicated Windows product with a 50 GB disk / 2 vCPU / 4 GB RAM minimum (the order form clamps to these), a BYOL acknowledgment, and standard linear compute pricing; it ships as Microsoft's 180-day evaluation and the customer applies their own Windows Server 2022 license (slmgr /ipk then /ato). os_template and gui_choice remain free-form strings resolved against the customer's eligible plan. v1.15.0 brings the documented OS/desktop catalog fully in line with the live, DB-driven /ordering/catalog: the orderable `template` option is debian13-cloud (default), debian12-cloud, ubuntu2404-cloud, ubuntu2204-cloud, fedora43-cloud, arch-cloud, nixos-cloud, almalinux9-cloud, alpine323-cloud, devuan5-cloud, openbsd79-cloud, and windows2022-byol; the `gui_choice` desktop option (AlmaLinux 9) is none/gnome/kde/xfce/cinnamon/mate. The catalog is authoritative and auto-reflects, so always read GET /ordering/catalog for the exact live set rather than relying on this prose. Additional images (ubuntu2604-cloud, freebsd14-cloud, openwrt-cloud, the NixOS 26.05 refresh, and Windows Server Core / Windows 11 desktop variants) are staged and will appear in the catalog as they are released.

## What's new in 2.4.6 (2026-07-13, non-breaking)

**Added**
- Customer-driven cloud-init operations are live: validate, confirm-gated apply, and confirm-gated revert via a pure-PHP NoCloud CD-ROM ISO seed. Apply creates a server-managed NoCloud seed, attaches it as the single CIDATA source, and keeps storage details hidden.
- Native agent session identity operations are live: create, list, get, confirm-gated revoke, and session audit.
- Agent sessions are customer-owned `shc_agent_` bearer tokens backed by server-side session records, bound to a Nostr public key, and require `X-User-Api-Agent-Proof` on every bearer request.
- Session scope is `read` or `operate`; read sessions are GET-only, operate sessions still cannot manage keys, password, 2FA, account identity, billing money movement, contacts, managers, or session material.
- Agent session audit records are customer-readable, attributed by sessionId and agentId, traceparent-aware, and carry tamper-evident event hashes under `data.tamperEvidence`.

## What's new in 2.4.4 (2026-07-13, non-breaking)

**Added**
- `GET /vm/{serviceId}/standby/preview` returns a read-only keep-IP vs release-IP standby quote from the standby quote contract.
- `POST /vm/{serviceId}/standby` parks an owned active VM through the standby contract with the requested `keep_ip` option. It is confirm-gated, requires `Idempotency-Key`, and returns state, `standby_recurring`, `park_credit`, and `ip_disposition`.
- `POST /vm/{serviceId}/resume` resumes an owned parked VM through the resume contract. It is confirm-gated, requires `Idempotency-Key`, and returns active state plus `resume_charge`.
- Standby/resume idempotency uses the header key and strict body replay. Same key plus same body replays the cached result; same key plus a different body returns 422 `idempotency_key_conflict`.


## What's new in 2.4.0 (2026-07-02, non-breaking)

**Fixed**
- Missing-VM lookups on `GET /vm/{service_id}/detail` and `POST /vm/{service_id}/ssh-keys/apply-live` now return **404** with `error_code: not_found` (previously 500).
- `GET /vm/{service_id}/summary` includes the live `runtime` object (state, lock, cpu_percent, mem_percent) — no second call to `/detail` needed for power state. If the hypervisor read fails, `runtime` is `null` and the summary still returns.
- All timestamps are now true UTC. Date fields such as `date_created` / `date_renews` were previously emitted 5 hours ahead; formats are unchanged (ISO-8601, `+00:00`).
- `GET /vm/{service_id}/activity` now shows a VM's own provisioning tasks (freshly created VMs no longer return an empty feed).
- Orders that cannot resolve an OS template are rejected up front with **422** `template_required`, and the message lists the package's valid template values. `config_options` with option ids that belong to a different package are rejected with **422** and the valid option ids are listed.
- API responses now begin with `{` — the stray plaintext banner line is gone. You can remove any strip-prefix workaround.

**Added**
- `error.error_code` on every error — a stable machine code (registry below) so clients no longer match on message text. Transient conditions also carry `error.retry_after_seconds`.
- 409 confirmation challenges mirror the id flat at `confirmation.confirmation_id` (all nested copies kept).
- Field aliases (dual-emitted, both accepted where written): `balances`/`credit` on the two balance endpoints, `items` alongside `upgradable` on upgrade-options, singular `service_id` on the order response, `id` (+ `snapshot_id` on snapshots) alongside `backup_id` on snapshot/backup items, `pricing_id` alongside `pricing_ref` on upgrades. Snapshot/backup write bodies accept `backup_id` | `snapshot_id` | `id` | `volid`.
- Cancel responses include top-level `expected_refund` and `transaction_id` (see the refund formula below).
- Firewall `action` is case-insensitive on input (always UPPERCASE in responses). `GET /ssh-key` accepts `?service_id=`. `POST /vm/{service_id}/console/session` accepts an optional `ttl` (seconds, clamped 5-300; default 30).
- `POST /agent-keys/claim` is documented: exchange a single-use claim code for an agent API key.
- MCP: `structuredContent.result` on every tool result (the unwrapped payload — uniform for reads and writes; the nested `data` shape is kept), a `listApiKeys` tool (full-scope keys may list key METADATA; creating and revoking keys stays Basic-auth-only), legacy tool-name aliases, and a `serverInfo.catalog` stamp (`spec_version`, `tool_count`).

### Machine error-code registry (`error.error_code`)

| error_code | Meaning | Retry |
|---|---|---|
| `not_found` | The addressed resource does not exist or is not owned by this account (404). | no |
| `invalid_token` | The API key was rejected (expired / revoked / unknown) — rotate or re-mint it. Missing credentials stay plain `unauthorized`. | after re-auth |
| `vm_locked` | The VM is transiently locked by another operation (for example a running backup). | yes — honor `retry_after_seconds` (default 15) |
| `insufficient_credit` | The requested amount exceeds the available balance (currently: payout requests; VM orders never hard-fail on credit — they invoice). | after top-up |
| `upstream_failure` | The hypervisor / storage backend could not service the request. | yes — honor `retry_after_seconds` (default 30) |
| `rate_limited` | Too many requests (429). | yes — honor `retry_after_seconds` |

Every other specific `error.code` value is mirrored into `error_code` verbatim. Sample error body:

```json
{"error": {"code": "conflict", "message": "Cancellation failed: ... VM is locked (backup) ...",
  "request_id": "1f0c...", "details": [], "error_code": "vm_locked", "retry_after_seconds": 15}}
```

### Cancellation refund formula

`expected_refund` / `cancel_credit.amount` come from ONE shared credit model (identical to the customer portal): for each paid invoice line covering the current term, credit = the line's cent-rounded amount x (unused seconds / covered seconds), summed in integer cents. A minimum charge of max(the term's 6-minute value, $0.01) is retained, already-issued this-term downgrade credits are subtracted, and the result is clamped to [0, amount paid]. End-of-term cancellations yield 0 (the term is fully used). The credit is issued as in-house account credit; `transaction_id` references it. Sample (1-day $0.24 term, canceled minutes after provisioning):

```json
{"data": {"id": 817, "service_status": "canceled", "expected_refund": 0.23, "transaction_id": 454,
  "cancel_credit": {"amount": 0.23, "currency": "USD", "transaction_id": 454, "issued": true}}}
```


## Request-body contracts & the MCP argument convention
Every operation that accepts a request body documents its exact fields (with a realistic example) in this spec's requestBody schema. How those fields map onto an MCP tool call (path/query params at the top level; the whole body under a single `body` object; the one `Idempotency-Key` header exception) is stated in the root `x-mcp-argument-convention` extension and in the docs page at `/user-api/docs/mcp-argument-convention.md`. Contracts are additive-only within a minor version; integrators may pin them. See `/user-api/CHANGELOG.md`.

mcp.note: The flagship MCP server exposes 169 eligible authenticated tools when requested with X-MCP-Tools: all; default tools/list returns 30 curated tools, and binary-response operations are REST-only. Spend and destructive tools use single-use confirmation_id gating. Authenticate with your customer shc_live_ operate key (Bearer). The /user-api/v2 REST base in servers is a supported legacy transport kept for backward compatibility; prefer MCP for new agent integrations.

x-agent-native.proprioception: Mutating responses carry a `next` object naming the single follow-up call that confirms the action: async producers (reinstall, backup/snapshot create+restore) return `next.poll` = /vm/{serviceId}/jobs/{jobId}; power verbs (start/stop/restart/shutdown/reset) return `expected_runtime_status` + `next.verify` = /vm/{serviceId}/summary; queued package-changes return `next.checkout_url`.
x-agent-native.error_contract: Errors follow a stable envelope {error:{code,message,request_id,details[]}}. Each detail MAY carry an optional stable `code` (required|invalid|already_in_use|out_of_range| unknown_field|unsupported_value) and a human/agent `hint`. On 429 the body MAY carry `error.retry_after_seconds` (mirrors the Retry-After header); errors MAY advertise `error.links[]` ({rel,href}) with rel in retry|status|docs|help|about.
x-agent-native.identifier_casing: operationIds are camelCase; resource identifiers in request/response BODIES are snake_case. Both styles are stable; do not transform one into the other.

operate_lane.summary: Obtain a SERVICE-SCOPED, cannot-spend operate Bearer from a customer Nostr grant (v2.0.1), no password shared.
operate_lane.request: Authorization: Nostr <base64 kind:27235 request>; body {grant: <customer-signed kind:30078 for your agent pubkey + one VM>}
operate_lane.result: A customer_key_operate Bearer scoped to the one granted VM; destructive (data-loss) and spend changes are confirm-gated (reinstall, restore/delete a backup or snapshot, cancel, delete a firewall rule, delete an SSH key, delete a contact/manager, ...) -- routine reads and non-destructive changes (power on/off/restart/shutdown/reset, ISO mount/unmount, firewall-rule add/edit, rDNS set, backup/snapshot create, add an SSH key, change backup schedule/notifications) are not. This mirrors the live customer portal (strict portal parity).

For keys minted since native area-scoping shipped this is an explicit array of aliases (an omitted 'areas' is materialized to the account's full permitted-area set, never stored unrestricted). A null value means a legacy key created before that change, which is treated as all areas (unrestricted).

post.summary: Create a scoped, expiring API key. The plaintext key is returned ONCE; store it now.

scope.description: read=GET only; operate=ops but no money/billing/credentials; full=all except credential management

areas.description: OPTIONAL. Native Blesta contact-permission area aliases this key may reach (ANDed with 'scope'). Omit to materialize the key to the account's FULL permitted-area set (the key is never stored area-unrestricted/null). Each value is validated against the account's native permission vocabulary; unknown areas are rejected 400, and a present-but-empty array is rejected 400 (omit the field instead). A key may only call operations whose x-required-area is in its set; identity/credential routes (x-required-area=__identity__) are never reachable by any key.

201.description: Created — body includes the plaintext key (shown once)

key.description: The secret API key — shown ONCE, never retrievable again.

post.description: Create a customer-managed scoped API key. Returns the plaintext key once. Optionally pass 'areas' (array of native contact-permission area aliases) to scope the key to a subset of areas; omit to materialize it to the account's full permitted-area set (the key is never stored area-unrestricted). See the customerApiKey security scheme and each operation's x-required-area.

parameters.name: confirm
parameters.description: The legacy ?confirm=true query flag is NOT accepted (the v2-native dispatcher rejects it). Destructive/spend actions use a server-issued, single-use confirmation_id two-step. First call this operation WITHOUT a confirmation_id: it returns 409 confirmation_required carrying a non-secret confirmation_id (bound server-side to this exact call + your caller fingerprint, single-use, short-TTL) plus a how_to_proceed re-call shape. After a human has approved the data loss, re-send the IDENTICAL request adding header X-User-Api-Confirm: <that confirmation_id> to execute. The confirmation_id carries no secret -- it only names an already-bound pending action, so a leaked or parroted id is inert (it can only complete the identical call by the same principal). A confirmation_id is NOT permission -- only re-send it after the human's explicit yes to THIS action. Power actions (start/stop/restart/shutdown/reset) are not destructive and need no confirmation.

post.summary: Add account credit (top up) via BTCPay
post.description: Mints a BTCPay payment with NO invoice attached, so the settled funds become unapplied **account credit** — the API equivalent of the customer portal's *Add Funds*. Returns a hosted `checkout_url` and, when available, a **BOLT11 Lightning invoice** (`bolt11`) and on-chain address so a wallet or automation can pay directly. Requires a full-scope customer API key or Basic+OTP; operate-scoped keys are rejected (this is a money-movement route, so an operate key cannot spend). The `amount` must be a positive decimal with at most 2 places (no rounding). Idempotent on the body `idempotency_key`. The credit lands once the BTCPay payment confirms (via webhook).

get.description: Returns the authenticated client's affiliate account: status, referral code and full referral link, lifetime stats (visits, sales, conversion rate), available Bitcoin balance, and the program terms (commission, withdrawal limits, cookie window). If the client is not enrolled, returns `{ "enrolled": false, "status": "not_enrolled", "eligible": <bool>, "program": {...} }` where `eligible` reflects the same active-service gate that `POST /affiliate/enroll` enforces. Self-scoped: the affiliate is resolved strictly from the authenticated client; no other affiliate is ever visible. Reports figures only; moves no money. Balances are BTC-native (8 decimal places); the BTC figures are not run through Blesta's FX layer.

post.summary: Claim a single-use agent API key by claim code
post.description: Public (unauthenticated) claim endpoint for operator-issued agent keys. The customer (or SHC on their behalf) mints a key and hands the AGENT only a single-use claim code; the agent exchanges the code here exactly once and receives the plaintext key (never recoverable again). Anti-enumeration: every existence-type miss (unknown / expired / already-claimed / raced) returns one uniform 404 body on a uniform response-time floor. Malformed requests are 422. Rate limited per source IP (30/10min) and per code (10/10min). NOT exposed as an MCP tool (public credential bootstrap; the MCP surface is authenticated).

expires_at.description: The KEY's own expiry (the claim itself is now spent).

404.description: Uniform miss: unknown, expired, already claimed, or raced. Indistinguishable by design.

422.description: Malformed request (missing/malformed code, or extra fields).

400.description: Missing/empty/non-object JSON body (dispatcher shape gate). Field-level problems (missing/malformed code, extra fields) are 422.

post.summary: Create a customer account and receive a usable API key in one anonymous call.
post.description: Anonymous, one-call customer signup. Creates a Blesta client account (the same client-creation path as the web order/registration form) and returns a pre-minted, scoped customer API key in the SAME 201 response, so a developer or AI agent goes from nothing to a usable account plus first API key without a second round-trip. No authentication is required (this is the only unauthenticated write endpoint documented here; the agent key-bootstrap /agent-keys/claim is the one other public route, an internal pairing endpoint). Email is the login identifier (no username). No CAPTCHA, no postal address, no phone, no email-verification gate. The new account is INERT until a first order is placed and its invoice is paid (registration provisions nothing and costs nothing). This endpoint is FRICTIONLESS and is NOT anti-enumeration: if the email is already registered it returns a plain 409 'email_exists' (this is intentional and accepted). The minted key cannot reach identity/credential routes regardless of scope (api-keys, password, 2FA, contact, and the primary-identity PATCH /account are Basic+OTP-only for every key). Rate limited per IP (global edge limit plus a tighter registration bucket). An optional Idempotency-Key header is honored at the rate-limit level only (full replay-storage is deferred for v1; a lost response is recovered by logging in to generate a key).

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Optional client-supplied idempotency key (16-128 chars). It is ACCEPTED for forward compatibility but has NO effect on /register in v1: registration is rate-limited by a per-IP bucket (5 per hour) that is ALWAYS consumed regardless of this header, and the key is deliberately NEVER folded into that bucket — if it were, rotating keys could mint a fresh slot each time and bypass the cap (this is intentional anti-bypass, not a limitation). The response is NOT stored or replayed, so reusing the key does NOT return the original 201; a duplicate email always returns 409 email_exists. Full byte-identical replay is deferred.

patch.summary: Change a VM package (upgrade/downgrade) — queued, awaiting payment
patch.description: Commits a package change to this service. MONEY MOVES: it creates a prorated invoice and a QUEUED change, then returns 202 Accepted. The VM is resized by Blesta's standard cron after the invoice is paid (it is never resized synchronously in this request). The request body MUST include idempotency_key (a missing key returns 400); reusing the same key with the same canonical body for the same service replays the original 202 response, and reusing it with a different body returns 409 idempotency_key_in_use. A disk-reducing change returns 422 (the module would destroy and recreate the VM). A change that requires no payment (zero or credit-only) returns 409 no_payable_change. Placement (node/host/storage/vmid) is never exposed.

post.summary: Rekey (rotate) a VM's zero-knowledge backup registration
post.description: Rotates the VM ZK backup recipient set forward for future backups while preserving self-custody for existing sealed backups. Requires X-User-Api-Confirm and ack=REKEY-WITH-RETENTION with selected currently-active recipient fingerprints carried forward into the submitted new set. SHC ZK backup is genuine self-custody, the same model as Bitcoin: your keys, your data. Backups already sealed to a recovery key stay openable by that key until you rotate forward and re-upload the backups you care about; that is the sovereignty property. If a recovery key is exposed, register a fresh recipient set and re-upload replacement backups, like sweeping a Bitcoin key to a fresh address. SHC cannot re-seal, claw back, or reach into existing backup data; that inability is the guarantee.

post.description: Submit an ordered array of live /user-api/v2 sub-requests. Responses are returned in the same order and echo each caller-supplied correlation id. Sub-requests are best-effort and independent: one failure is reported in that item and does not roll back successful siblings. Every sub-request is dispatched through the same native guard chain as a standalone call, including authenticated principal, bearer scope, per-operation confirmation, ownership, rate accounting, and per-operation idempotency. A batch consumes one rate unit per sub-request. The wrapper itself requires Idempotency-Key; replaying the same key and body returns the cached BatchResponse without re-executing sub-requests. If any sub-request targets a confirmation-gated operation, the batch wrapper must also include X-User-Api-Confirm as an explicit batch-level acknowledgement; per-sub-request confirm values are still required for guarded sub-requests.

x-shc-batch.per_subrequest_confirm: confirm maps to X-User-Api-Confirm for that sub-request only
x-shc-batch.per_subrequest_idempotency: idempotencyKey maps to Idempotency-Key for that sub-request only
x-shc-batch.guard_inheritance: scope, ownership, confirmation, destructive, money, rate, and idempotency guards are evaluated against the target operation exactly as direct calls
x-shc-batch.handler_build: native batch dispatcher on the live v2 surface
x-shc-batch.batch_idempotency: Idempotency-Key is required on POST /batch and caches the whole BatchResponse
x-shc-batch.rate_accounting: a batch of N sub-requests consumes N request-rate units
x-shc-batch.no_nested_batch: sub-requests resolving to /batch or submitBatch are rejected

post.summary: Register a VM ZK backup recipient set
post.description: Registers the initial customer-controlled ZK backup config and full recipient set. Requires X-User-Api-Confirm. SHC receives only public recipient material and KDF parameters, never private keys or passphrases. This starts the customer's self-custody recipient set; use rotate-forward rekey for future recipient changes.

201.description: ZK backup status for the owned VM.

get.description: Returns verified ZK backup status for one owned VM. SHC ZK backup is genuine self-custody, the same model as Bitcoin: your keys, your data. Backups already sealed to a recovery key stay openable by that key until you rotate forward and re-upload the backups you care about; that is the sovereignty property. If a recovery key is exposed, register a fresh recipient set and re-upload replacement backups, like sweeping a Bitcoin key to a fresh address. SHC cannot re-seal, claw back, or reach into existing backup data; that inability is the guarantee.

internalToken.description: Internal SHC bearer token for AI bots and service-to-service calls. Tokens are audience-scoped and may be authorized for only a subset of audited operations. For example, the `contextvm_support_runtime` audience is limited to GET `/account`, GET `/vm/templates`, GET `/vm/{service_id}`, GET `/vm/{service_id}/backups`, GET `/vm/{service_id}/snapshots`, GET `/vm/{service_id}/jobs`, and GET `/vm/{service_id}/jobs/{job_id}`. This internal-token scheme is for staff/system callers only. Customers do NOT use it; a customer authenticates with HTTP Basic (+OTP) or with a customer-minted API key (the separate customerApiKey scheme, Authorization: Bearer shc_live_...). Successful and failed internal-token requests are audit logged.

customerApiKey.description: A customer-managed scoped API key: Authorization: Bearer shc_live_... . Create/list/revoke at /account/api-keys (Basic+OTP only). Two independent authorization axes are ANDed (both must pass): (1) METHOD/scope: read (GET only), operate (no money/billing/credential ops), full (all except credential management); (2) AREA: an OPTIONAL subset of Blesta native contact-permission areas. Each operation declares its governing area via x-required-area. When 'areas' is omitted (the default) the key is NOT stored unrestricted: it is materialized to an explicit CSV of the account's own permitted areas (for the account owner that is every alias below), so it reaches all areas you can access while never carrying a NULL/all-areas scope. A key created with an 'areas' array may only reach operations whose x-required-area is in that set. Area aliases: client_invoices, client_quotations, client_services, client_transactions, client_contacts, client_accounts, client_emails, _credits, _managed, support. Identity/credential routes (x-required-area=__identity__: /account/api-keys*, /account/password, /account/2fa*, /account/contact, PATCH /account) are NEVER reachable by ANY key (Basic+OTP only), regardless of scope or areas. Area scope never substitutes for tenant isolation: client_id ownership is always enforced server-side.

agentSessionBearer.description: Customer-minted agent session token: Authorization: Bearer shc_agent_... . Every request using this token must also include X-User-Api-Agent-Proof containing a base64url-encoded NIP-98 kind 27235 event signed by the session publicKey and bound to the exact request method and URL. Sessions are read or operate scoped and can never manage keys, passwords, 2FA, account identity, billing money movement, contacts, managers, or agent-session material.

The available option IDs, labels, and allowed values for each plan are listed under `config_options` in GET /ordering/catalog. Example: selecting the +1 TB disk add-on whose option ID is 15 → {"15": "1024"}. Omit to take the plan defaults. The Operating System and Desktop Environment are themselves configurable options: the `template` option (label "Operating System") selects the OS image (e.g. debian13-cloud, debian12-cloud, ubuntu2404-cloud, ubuntu2204-cloud, fedora43-cloud, arch-cloud, nixos-cloud, almalinux9-cloud, alpine323-cloud, devuan5-cloud, openbsd79-cloud, windows2022-byol), and the `gui_choice` option (label "Desktop Environment", AlmaLinux 9) selects an optional desktop (none, gnome, kde, xfce, cinnamon, mate). Their option IDs and allowed values are listed under `config_options` in GET /ordering/catalog. IMPORTANT: a VM order requires a resolved operating-system template. If the selected package does not define a default OS template, you MUST include the `template` option in config_options (use the option ID and an allowed value from GET /ordering/catalog); otherwise the order is rejected at submission with 400 error code `template_required` (no pending service/invoice is created). transaction_id.description: Blesta transaction id for the issued in_house_credit (transaction_type_id=4). `null` if no credit was issued (zero amount or remediation failure).

issued.description: True only when an in_house_credit transaction was successfully recorded.

expected_refund.description: v2.4.0 (additive, top-level mirror): the issued unused-time credit in the billing currency, computed by the SAME shared cancel-credit model as cancel_credit.amount (see the refund formula in the API description). 0 for end-of-term cancels and when nothing was due.

transaction_id.description: v2.4.0 (additive, top-level mirror of cancel_credit.transaction_id): the in_house_credit transaction id, null when no credit was issued or the cancel was end-of-term.

CancelVmResponseData.description: VmDetail extended with `cancel_credit` when the cancel was immediate. End-of-term cancels return the bare VmDetail shape (no `cancel_credit`). v2.4.0: top-level expected_refund + transaction_id mirrors are always present on cancel responses.

backup_id.description: Opaque, per-customer backup/restore-point handle (`bk_…`). Returned in place of the real storage volume id so the underlying Proxmox vmid/node is never disclosed. Use this value verbatim as the restore/delete/protection/verify/file-restore/restore-hints handle; it is mapped back to the real volume server-side.

storage.description: Datastore class label with any per-node suffix removed (never reveals the host node).

created_epoch.description: Unix timestamp (seconds since 1970-01-01 UTC). Surfaced alongside `created_at` for clients that prefer a numeric value.

id.description: v2.4.0 alias (additive): identical to backup_id (the opaque handle).

snapshot_id.description: v2.4.0 alias (additive): present on kind=snapshot items only; identical to backup_id. Write bodies accept backup_id | snapshot_id | id | volid.

ProxmoxJobRequested.description: Normalized request fields captured when the job was queued.

step.description: Current/last human-readable progress step. Sanitized at the API boundary so it never discloses the real Proxmox vmid, host node, or paths.

encryption_key.description: Reserved — client-side backup encryption is not yet available; a non-empty value returns 501.

RenewQuote.description: Renewal quote for one existing service.

gateway.description: Gateway class selector. Only the enabled BTCPay nonmerchant gateway is currently accepted; other values return 400.

idempotency_key.description: Invoice-scoped idempotency key. Reuse the same value with the same body to replay the original response for this invoice.

return_url.description: Optional HTTPS URL to which BTCPay should redirect the browser after checkout. Non-https URLs are rejected with 400.

cancel_url.description: Optional HTTPS URL accepted for client compatibility. BTCPay's native Blesta flow uses a single redirect URL, so this value is advisory unless the gateway gains first-class support for it. Non-https URLs are rejected with 400.

transaction_id.description: Blesta transaction row ID when one exists. Zero-value invoices may close without a backing transaction row.

applied_credit.description: Amount of client credit applied by this call. `0.00` for zero-value invoices and already-paid invoices.

ssh_key.description: The single-line public key. Multi-key historical values are split into one entry per line.

key_fingerprint.description: Server-computed SHA-256 fingerprint in OpenSSH form (`SHA256:` + base64 with padding stripped). Pass this verbatim to `DELETE /ssh-key` to remove the entry. May be `null` for keys that fail to parse server-side.

ssh_key.description: Single-line ssh-rsa, ssh-ed25519, or ecdsa-sha2-nistp256/384/521 public key.

message.example: SSH key saved. It will be applied on next reinstall.

example.message: SSH key saved. It will be applied on next reinstall.

key_fingerprint.description: Fingerprint of the stored SSH public key to remove. `SHA256:` and `MD5:` forms are accepted.

fingerprint.description: Present when a matching key was removed.

message.description: Present for idempotent no-op deletes when the fingerprint is not stored.

VmSummaryList.description: Canonical paginated list of customer VM summaries.

PlanList.description: Canonical paginated list of descriptive VM plans. Each item embeds its default image metadata when available.

OrderablePlanList.description: Canonical paginated list of orderable VM plans.

TemplateList.description: Canonical paginated list of VM templates/images.

StorageItemList.description: Canonical paginated list of backup or snapshot items.

ProxmoxJobList.description: Canonical paginated list of background jobs.

InvoiceSummaryList.description: Canonical paginated list of invoice summaries.

InvoiceLineList.description: Canonical paginated list of invoice line items.

SshKeyEntryList.description: Canonical paginated list of stored SSH keys.

AppliedPayment.description: A transaction applied to an invoice (shown when inv_display_payments is enabled).

AccountInvoiceDetail.description: Customer-safe single invoice with line items and optional applied payments.

applied_payments.description: Present only when the inv_display_payments company setting is enabled.

credit.description: v2.4.0 alias (additive): per-currency available credit as {currency, amount} — the same list /account/balance calls 'credit'.

EmailSummary.description: Customer-safe email / notice list row (internal columns stripped).

body_preview.description: First ~280 characters of the plain-text body.

EmailDetail.description: Customer-safe email / notice detail with full body.

VmIso.description: ISO images and CD-ROM state for an owned VM.

mounted.description: CD-ROM drives currently mounted on the VM.

volid.description: Proxmox volume id of the mounted ISO.

available.description: ISO images available to mount (excludes those already mounted).

size_bytes.description: ISO size in bytes, if reported.

boot_order.description: Current boot order (read-only; changed only via mount/unmount).

VmFirewall.description: Per-VM firewall configuration and rule-form vocabulary.

policy_in.description: Default inbound policy (ACCEPT, DROP, or REJECT).

policy_out.description: Default outbound policy (ACCEPT, DROP, or REJECT).

interfaces.description: VM network interface keys (e.g. net0) selectable on a rule.

items.description: v2.4.0: case-INSENSITIVE on input (accept/Drop/REJECT all valid); always canonical UPPERCASE on output.

icmp_types.description: Supported ICMP types for ICMP rules.

VmFirewallRule.description: A single firewall rule. Field names mirror the Proxmox rule object as consumed by the client portal; 'type' is the rule direction (in/out).

pos.description: Rule position (ordering key). Positions renumber when a rule is deleted.

enable.description: 1 if the rule is enabled, 0 otherwise.

VmMetricSeries.description: One RRD metric series. values is an array of [epoch_seconds, value] pairs. divider (when present) converts raw values to display units.

prefixItems.description: Sample value (raw; apply divider for display units). CPU is a 0-1 fraction.

divider.description: Divide raw values by this for display units (RAM 1073741824=GiB; disk/network 1048576=MiB). Absent for CPU.

rate.description: Shaping rate in Mbit, or null when unshaped.

VmRuntimeOverview.description: Live power-state snapshot (subset of the Proxmox status/current; host-identifying fields are omitted).

state.description: raw_status, or 'locked' when a lock is held.

lock.description: Lock reason when locked (e.g. backup, migrate, snapshot).

VmPciSummary.description: GPU/PCI passthrough summary card. Only the device count and a primary label/short are exposed; per-device topology (pci_id, vendor:device, IOMMU group) is intentionally withheld.

primary.description: Null when no PCI devices are assigned.

VmActivityItem.description: One Proxmox task. Infrastructure identifiers (UPID/node/PID/PVE user) are stripped for tenant isolation.

status.description: PVE task exit status ('OK' on success), or 'running' when not yet finished.

PermissionOptionsEnvelope.description: Vocabulary of permission areas that may be granted to a contact or account manager, as key/label pairs. Returned by GET /contacts/permission-options and GET /managers/permission-options (the manager vocabulary is the contact vocabulary minus the _managed area).

permissions.description: Grantable permission areas. Keys mirror the portal's permission options, including any plugin-contributed areas.

label.description: Human-readable label for the permission area.

QuotationSummary.description: Compact view of one quotation owned by the authenticated client. Money fields are formatted as fixed-point strings (two decimal places) to preserve precision. Returned directly in the GET /quotations list; the GET /quotations/{quotation_id} detail response composes this summary with notes and line_items via allOf.

id_code.description: Formatted quotation number shown to the customer.

subtotal.description: Quotation subtotal before tax, as a fixed-point string with two decimal places.

total.description: Quotation grand total, as a fixed-point string with two decimal places.

currency.description: ISO-4217 currency code for the quotation amounts.

VmIsoMountRequest.description: Mount an ISO image as a new CD-ROM drive. The ISO must be one offered by GET /vm/{service_id}/iso and not already mounted.

iso.description: Proxmox volume id of the ISO to mount (from the `available` list of GET /vm/{service_id}/iso).

VmIsoUnmountRequest.description: Unmount a mounted ISO CD-ROM drive. Idempotent: an absent/already-unmounted drive returns 200 with the refreshed list.

drive.description: QEMU IDE cdrom drive key to detach (from the `mounted` list of GET /vm/{service_id}/iso), e.g. ide2.

VmDataPreferencesUpdateRequest.description: Update backup/snapshot schedule, retention, notification, and encryption-pubkey preferences. PATCH semantics: only the sections/keys present are changed. At least one recognized field is expected. If zk_backup is supplied for first registration, X-User-Api-Confirm is required before the initial recipient set is written.

backup.description: Backup or snapshot scheduling preferences. Only the keys present are updated (PATCH semantics).

retention.description: A configured retention preset key, the literal `keep-all`, or a comma-separated `keep-<unit>=<n>` policy (units: last, hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, yearly).

auto_days.description: Days the automatic job runs (configured schedule-day keys).

auto_time.description: Hour the automatic job runs, in `HH:00` form.

snapshot.description: Backup or snapshot scheduling preferences. Only the keys present are updated (PATCH semantics).

retention.description: A configured retention preset key, the literal `keep-all`, or a comma-separated `keep-<unit>=<n>` policy (units: last, hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, yearly).

auto_days.description: Days the automatic job runs (configured schedule-day keys).

auto_time.description: Hour the automatic job runs, in `HH:00` form.

notify.description: Notification toggles. Each accepts a JSON boolean or the strings on/off/true/false/1/0.

encryption_pubkey.description: Reserved — client-side backup encryption is not yet available; a non-empty value returns 501.

zk_backup.description: Legacy first-registration branch. Prefer POST /vm/{serviceId}/zk-backup/register. Requires X-User-Api-Confirm and must be sent without other data-preferences fields.

VmFirewallRuleCreateRequest.description: Create a firewall rule. `action` and `direction` are required; all other fields are optional. The raw Proxmox `type` key is rejected (use `direction`).

action.description: Rule action. v2.4.0: case-INSENSITIVE on input (accept/Drop/REJECT all valid); always canonical UPPERCASE on output.

direction.description: Rule direction. (Security-group rules cannot be created/edited via the API.)

source.description: Source address: IP, CIDR, or an IPSet/alias name (comma-separated tokens allowed).

source_port.description: Source port: a port, a `lo:hi` range, a comma list, or a service name.

dest.description: Destination address: IP, CIDR, or an IPSet/alias name.

dest_port.description: Destination port: a port, a `lo:hi` range, a comma list, or a service name.

protocol.description: Protocol name (tcp, udp, icmp, ...) or a numeric protocol id 0-255.

macro.description: A firewall macro name from GET /vm/{service_id}/firewall, or `none` for no macro.

interface.description: Bind to a VM network interface name (e.g. net0).

icmp-type.description: ICMP type, from the firewall vocabulary (GET /vm/{service_id}/firewall `icmp_types`).

enabled.description: Whether the rule is enabled. Accepts a JSON boolean or on/off/true/false/1/0. Defaults to true on create; on edit, omitting it keeps the current state.

VmFirewallRuleUpdateRequest.description: Edit a firewall rule by position. PATCH semantics: only the supplied fields change; at least one recognized field is required. `action`/`direction` are NOT required on an edit. The raw Proxmox `type` key is rejected.

action.description: Rule action. v2.4.0: case-INSENSITIVE on input (accept/Drop/REJECT all valid); always canonical UPPERCASE on output.

direction.description: Rule direction. (Security-group rules cannot be created/edited via the API.)

source.description: Source address: IP, CIDR, or an IPSet/alias name (comma-separated tokens allowed).

source_port.description: Source port: a port, a `lo:hi` range, a comma list, or a service name.

dest.description: Destination address: IP, CIDR, or an IPSet/alias name.

dest_port.description: Destination port: a port, a `lo:hi` range, a comma list, or a service name.

protocol.description: Protocol name (tcp, udp, icmp, ...) or a numeric protocol id 0-255.

macro.description: A firewall macro name from GET /vm/{service_id}/firewall, or `none` for no macro.

interface.description: Bind to a VM network interface name (e.g. net0).

icmp-type.description: ICMP type, from the firewall vocabulary (GET /vm/{service_id}/firewall `icmp_types`).

enabled.description: Whether the rule is enabled. Accepts a JSON boolean or on/off/true/false/1/0. Defaults to true on create; on edit, omitting it keeps the current state.

VmFirewallRuleListResponse.description: The refreshed client-visible firewall rule list after a rule mutation (security-group rules excluded).

VmFirewallPolicyUpdateRequest.description: Set the default inbound/outbound firewall policy. PATCH semantics: only the keys present are set; at least one of policy_in/policy_out is required.

policy_in.description: Default inbound policy. v2.4.0: case-INSENSITIVE on input (accept/Drop/REJECT all valid); always canonical UPPERCASE on output.

policy_out.description: Default outbound policy. v2.4.0: case-INSENSITIVE on input (accept/Drop/REJECT all valid); always canonical UPPERCASE on output.

VmFirewallPolicyResponse.description: The refreshed default firewall policy after an update.

VmSshKeyApplyLiveRequest.description: Live-inject a single-line SSH public key into the running VM via the guest agent. The key is also persisted so a later reinstall keeps it.

ssh_key.description: Single-line ssh-rsa, ssh-ed25519, or ecdsa-sha2-nistp256/384/521 public key.

VmSshKeyApplyLiveResponse.description: Result of a live SSH-key inject. The live guest-exec result is best-effort and not authoritatively confirmable, so `live_inject` is always `attempted` (never `success`).

key_fingerprint.description: Server-computed SHA-256 fingerprint of the injected key (pass to DELETE /vm/{service_id}/ssh-keys/live to live-remove it).

VmSshKeyDeleteLiveRequest.description: Live-remove an SSH key from the running VM by fingerprint, and scrub it from the persisted set.

key_fingerprint.description: Fingerprint of the key to remove. `SHA256:` and `MD5:` forms are accepted.

VmSshKeyDeleteLiveResponse.description: Result of a live SSH-key removal. Idempotent: `removed` is false when the fingerprint was not present.

VmStorageVerifyRequest.description: Acknowledge a verify request for an owned backup/snapshot volume. The backup_id must exist in this VM's own inventory. v2.4.0: exactly one of backup_id | snapshot_id | id | volid is required (aliases, first present wins).

backup_id.description: Opaque, per-customer backup/restore-point handle (`bk_…`). Returned in place of the real storage volume id so the underlying Proxmox vmid/node is never disclosed. Use this value verbatim as the restore/delete/protection/verify/file-restore/restore-hints handle; it is mapped back to the real volume server-side.

snapshot_id.description: v2.4.0 alias of backup_id (accepted on input).

id.description: v2.4.0 alias of backup_id (accepted on input).

VmStorageVerifyResponse.description: Synchronous no-op verify acknowledgement (no background job is queued).

backup_id.description: Opaque, per-customer backup/restore-point handle (`bk_…`). Returned in place of the real storage volume id so the underlying Proxmox vmid/node is never disclosed. Use this value verbatim as the restore/delete/protection/verify/file-restore/restore-hints handle; it is mapped back to the real volume server-side.

SupportTicketAttachment.description: A base64-encoded file to attach to a ticket or reply.

SupportTicketCustomFields.description: Department custom-field values keyed by numeric field id. Values are scalar (string/number/boolean).

SupportTicketCreateRequest.description: Create a support ticket. `department_id`, `subject`, and `message` are required.

department_id.description: Target support department (must be visible to this client; see GET /support/departments).

subject.example: Cannot reach my VM over SSH

message.example: My VM stopped responding to SSH around 14:00 UTC.

priority.description: Priority key valid for the department. Defaults to the department's default priority.

attachments.description: Optional base64-encoded attachments (subject to a total-size cap).

example.subject: Cannot reach my VM over SSH
example.message: My VM stopped responding to SSH around 14:00 UTC.

SupportTicketReplyRequest.description: Add a reply to an owned ticket. Replying to a closed/awaiting-reply ticket reopens it.

CreditHandlingUpdateRequest.description: Full replacement of the per-currency low-credit notification threshold map. An empty `thresholds` object clears all thresholds.

thresholds.description: Map of ISO-4217 currency code to threshold amount. Each currency must exist for the company.

AccountPreferencesUpdateRequest.description: Update self-scoped, company-editable client preferences. PATCH semantics: only the keys present are changed; at least one field is required. Only fields the company has flagged editable (see GET /account/preferences `editable`) may be changed. `autodebit` is not writable through this API.

inv_address_to.description: Contact id to address invoices to (must be one of this client's contacts).

ManagedAccountInvitationRequest.description: Accept or decline a management invitation addressed to the authenticated client.

action.description: Whether to accept or decline the invitation.

ManagedAccountInvitationResponse.description: Result of accepting/declining a management invitation.

managed_client_id.description: The client id this client now manages (on accept) or that the invitation referenced.

already_resolved.description: Present and true when accept is a no-op because the invitation was already accepted (idempotent).

ManagerDeleteResponse.description: Result of revoking an active manager (returns contact_id) or declining a pending invitation (returns status=declined).

contact_id.description: Present when an active manager (numeric ref) was revoked.

status.description: Present (value `declined`) when a pending invitation (token ref) was cancelled.

ActivityItem.description: One customer-visible account activity event, merged most-recent-first from Blesta's native logs (sign-ins, failed sign-ins, account contact-detail changes, account-setting changes, and payment/transaction activity). Polymorphic on `type`: only the fields relevant to that event kind are populated; type-specific fields are null/absent for other types. NEVER exposes internal placement (node/VMID/host), staff identity, or the old/new VALUES of changed fields.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC time of the event; null if the source datetime was zero (0000-00-00 00:00:00).

timestamp_epoch.description: Unix epoch of the event (the sort key); null if the source datetime was zero.

ip_address.description: login / login_failed only: the customer's OWN source IP. Null for other event types.

changed_fields.description: contact_change / setting_change only: the NAMES of the changed fields (allow-listed). Field names only — never the previous/new values. Empty for other event types.

changed_count.description: contact_change/setting_change: the TOTAL number of changed fields (a count only). May exceed the length of changed_fields, which lists only the allow-listed, customer-safe field NAMES.

amount.description: transaction only: 2-decimal amount string. Null for other event types.

currency.description: transaction only: ISO-4217 currency code. Null for other event types.

status.description: transaction only: transaction status. Null for other event types.

AccountActivityList.description: Canonical paginated list of account activity events.

VmUpgradeConfigOptionCatalog.description: A settable/addable config option for an upgradable plan, identical in shape to the ordering catalog (option_id is the raw package option id).

VmUpgradeOptionTerm.description: One client-orderable term for an upgradable plan. pricing_ref is the raw package_pricing id to pass to preview/PATCH.

recurring_amount.description: Recurring price (base plan) at this term.

setup_fee.description: One-time setup fee at this term.

VmUpgradablePlan.description: A same-group package the service may move to. disk_reduces / disk_change_blocked flag a base-disk-shrinking move (rejected at commit). Placement is never present.

disk_gb.description: Base package disk (GB), config options excluded.

available_config_options.description: Catalog of settable config options per term (ordering catalog shape).

disk_reduces.description: True if a no-add-on move to this plan would reduce disk.

disk_change_blocked.description: True if a disk-reducing move is rejected at commit.

VmUpgradeOptionsResponse.description: The current plan + the same-group plans the customer may move to, with a blocked_reason when a change cannot proceed. Placement is never exposed.

disk_gb.description: Current effective disk (base meta + current disk options).

blocked_reason.description: Why a change cannot proceed right now, or null.

items.description: v2.4.0 alias (additive): identical to 'upgradable' (the generic list key).

VmUpgradePreviewRequest.description: Target pricing_ref (raw package_pricing.id) + optional config_options to price. v2.4.0: one of pricing_ref | pricing_id is required (aliases; pricing_ref wins if both sent).

pricing_ref.description: Raw package_pricing.id (same-group, client-allowable, same billing term).

config_options.description: Map of package option id (string) -> selected value, validated identically to ordering.

pricing_id.description: v2.4.0 alias (additive): synonym of pricing_ref. pricing_ref wins if both are sent.

VmUpgradePreviewResponse.description: Prorated quote (no charge). amount_due_now already includes any setup fee. applies is always "queued" (the change is created queued, awaiting payment).

prorate_credit.description: Prorated credit for a downgrade, only when account credit is enabled (else 0.00).

amount_due_now.description: Amount due now (includes setup fee). 0.00 when nothing is owed.

new_recurring_amount.description: New recurring amount = base renewal + config-option recurring totals.

setup_fee_total.description: Discrete setup-fee subtotal, only when the presenter exposes it (already in amount_due_now).

VmUpgradeRequest.description: Target pricing_ref + optional config_options + the REQUIRED idempotency_key. v2.4.0: one of pricing_ref | pricing_id is required (aliases; pricing_ref wins if both sent).

pricing_ref.description: Raw package_pricing.id (same-group, client-allowable, same billing term).

config_options.description: Map of package option id (string) -> selected value (validated identically to ordering).

idempotency_key.description: REQUIRED. Service-scoped idempotency key (a missing key returns 400). Reuse the same value with the same body to replay the original 202 for this service.

pricing_id.description: v2.4.0 alias (additive): synonym of pricing_ref (the RAW package_pricing.id, the same id the ordering catalog calls pricing_id). pricing_ref wins if both are sent.

VmUpgradeResponse.description: Result of an accepted (queued) package change. change is always "queued"; the prorated invoice_id is awaiting payment and the change is applied by cron once that invoice is paid.

amount.description: Amount due on the prorated invoice.

new_recurring_amount.description: New recurring amount (base + ALL merged config-option recurring).

amount.description: Amount of account credit to add. Send a positive decimal STRING with at most 2 decimal places (e.g. "25.00"); a bare integer is also accepted. A JSON float is REJECTED (422) so the customer is charged EXACTLY what they type — no silent rounding. Must be > 0 and within the account's configured credit limits.

currency.description: Optional ISO-4217 code (defaults to the client's currency). Must be an active company currency.

return_url.description: Optional HTTPS URL BTCPay redirects the browser to after payment. Non-https or non-allowlisted hosts are rejected with 400.

idempotency_key.description: REQUIRED client-supplied idempotency key (body field). Reuse the same value with the same body to replay the original response instead of minting a second top-up invoice.

amount.description: The credited amount as a 2-decimal string.

checkout_url.description: BTCPay hosted checkout page (pay by Lightning or on-chain in a browser).

bolt11.description: A BOLT11 Lightning invoice for the amount, for wallets/automation that pay without the hosted page. Null if a direct Lightning invoice is unavailable for this invoice.

onchain_address.description: On-chain Bitcoin address, when the store offers on-chain. Null on a Lightning-only store.

payment_link.description: A single wallet-openable payment URI (prefers lightning:lnbc…, else lightning:lnurl…, else bitcoin:…).

email.description: The account login identifier (used as the HTTP Basic username). Must be a valid email address.

password.description: Account password. Minimum 8 characters; no other complexity rule is imposed.

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (case-insensitive; stored uppercased). Optional; defaults to US. Validated against the supported country list.

recovery_email.description: Optional alternate email for password-reset delivery.

scope.description: Optional scope for the API key minted at registration. Defaults to 'operate' (read + provision, never spend). The anonymous /register endpoint is capped to {read, operate}; full keys are issued only via your account settings (authenticated POST /account/api-keys) after you sign in. Note that no scope can reach identity/credential routes (api-keys, password, 2FA, contact, primary-identity PATCH /account) — those are Basic+OTP-only for every key.

RegisterApiKey.description: A customer API key minted at registration. The plaintext `key` is shown ONCE.

ContactCreateRequest.description: Fields for creating a client-owned contact. Mirrors the portal add-contact form.

ZkBackupRegistration.description: Zero-knowledge backup registration: client-derived X25519 pubkeys + immutable KDF config. Exactly one recipient must be kind=password (the primary). The server never sees the password or private keys.

config.description: Immutable per-service KDF config; salt is 16 client-random bytes (lowercase hex).

kind.description: How this recipient's X25519 public key was derived — what the customer holds to unlock a restore (five-rung recovery model; any one recipient suffices): `password` — a passphrase (argon2id-derived key). `recovery-key` — a generated high-entropy recovery code the customer stored. `passkey` — a FIDO2 passkey using the PRF extension. `shamir` — one share-set of a split secret (threshold recovery). `ssh-ed25519` — an existing Ed25519 SSH key (converted). `secp256k1` — a secp256k1 key such as a Nostr identity (the input alias `nostr` is accepted and normalized to `secp256k1`). `pgp` — an OpenPGP key. `btc` — a Bitcoin key. `other` — any other X25519-capable material the customer manages.

pubkey.description: X25519 public key (hex); private key never leaves the client.

mlkem_pubkey.description: ML-KEM-1024 public key hex for pq-hybrid recipients. Public key only; private key never leaves the customer.

reader_id.description: Derived by SHC as SHA256(pubkey_x25519_hex || mlkem_pubkey) and returned in recipient status.

keep_ip.description: When true, keep the currently assigned IP through standby. Default false releases the IP while parked.

ZkBackupRecipientStatus.description: One public ZK backup recipient row in the verified recipient set. Public keys and fingerprints are owner-visible; private keys are never present.

ZkBackupRecipientSetStatus.description: Verified owner-visible ZK backup recipient set, including revoked recipients. SHC ZK backup is genuine self-custody, the same model as Bitcoin: your keys, your data. Backups already sealed to a recovery key stay openable by that key until you rotate forward and re-upload the backups you care about; that is the sovereignty property. If a recovery key is exposed, register a fresh recipient set and re-upload replacement backups, like sweeping a Bitcoin key to a fresh address. SHC cannot re-seal, claw back, or reach into existing backup data; that inability is the guarantee.

revocation_guarantee.example: SHC ZK backup is genuine self-custody, the same model as Bitcoin: your keys, your data. Backups already sealed to a recovery key stay openable by that key until you rotate forward and re-upload the backups you care about; that is the sovereignty property. If a recovery key is exposed, register a fresh recipient set and re-upload replacement backups, like sweeping a Bitcoin key to a fresh address. SHC cannot re-seal, claw back, or reach into existing backup data; that inability is the guarantee.

ack.description: Acknowledges rotate-forward self-custody: revocation stops future seals to this recipient, while already sealed backups stay openable by their sealed recovery keys until customer re-upload.
Acknowledges rotate-forward self-custody: future backups use the new recipient set and existing backups stay openable by their sealed recovery keys until customer re-upload.

retain_fingerprints.description: Currently-active recipient fingerprints that must be carried forward into the submitted new recipient set.

ZkBackupRetentionRekeyRequest.description: Rotate-forward ZK backup rekey request. Future backups use the submitted recipient set; existing backups stay openable by their sealed recovery keys until the customer re-uploads the backups they care about.

IdempotencyKey.description: Effective idempotency key for this order submission, echoed from the request or generated by the server.

Link.description: RFC 8288 typed web links. Cursor-paginated feeds use rel="next" when another page exists.

CacheControlHeader.description: RFC 9111 cache directive selected from the deterministic v3 volatility class for this GET response.

PreferenceApplied.description: RFC 7240 Preference-Applied response header. The value is a comma-separated list of applied preferences. Mutations echo dry-run when a real no-commit preview is returned and ignore return-shape tokens. GET responses echo return=minimal or return=representation when honored; dry-run is inapplicable on GETs.

ETagHeader.description: RFC 7232 entity-tag for the canonical response representation computed by the v3 response-envelope shim. The value may be strong or weak.

Vary.description: RFC 9110 Vary header. Cacheable GET responses that honor response-shape Prefer vary on Prefer and Accept-Encoding. fields remains part of the canonical request URI and ETag input, not a Vary field.

x-mcp-argument-convention.summary: How an MCP tool's arguments map onto this REST API's request. Every MCP tool inputSchema is generated 1:1 from this OpenAPI document by McpCatalog::buildInputSchema; pin against this document and this convention.
x-mcp-argument-convention.top_level_arguments: Path and query parameters become TOP-LEVEL tool arguments, named verbatim as they appear in this spec. Path parameters are live lowerCamelCase (serviceId, invoiceId, ticketId, contactId, ...). Query parameters keep live handler names; most are camelCase, with documented exceptions such as category_id.
x-mcp-argument-convention.request_body: The ENTIRE requestBody is passed as a single nested object argument named body. Its fields are exactly the requestBody schema fields in this spec, in live /v2 snake_case. When requestBody.required is true, body is a required argument.
x-mcp-argument-convention.header_exception: HTTP headers are NOT tool arguments, with ONE exception: Idempotency-Key becomes a top-level argument literally named Idempotency-Key only for createVirtualMachineOrder, buyVirtualMachine, requestAffiliatePayout, standbyVirtualMachine, resumeVirtualMachine, updateVirtualMachineCloudInit, and deleteVirtualMachineCloudInit.
x-mcp-argument-convention.strictness: The tool inputSchema is additionalProperties:false and performs NO snake_case<->camelCase coercion. An unknown TOP-LEVEL key is rejected by the MCP binder ('Unexpected argument supplied'); an unknown BODY field is rejected by the REST handler ("Unknown field '<x>'"). Put path/query args at the top level and ALL request-body fields under 'body'.
x-mcp-argument-convention.id_placement: Deleting or modifying a sub-resource by id is modeled two ways in this API and both are intentional (see the per-operation x-shc-id-placement extension): firewall RULES take the id as a PATH parameter (pos) with no body; backups/snapshots take the id as a BODY field (backup_id, with snapshot_id/id accepted as aliases).
x-mcp-argument-convention.idempotency_placement: Idempotency is an optional HEADER (Idempotency-Key) for createVirtualMachineOrder, buyVirtualMachine, and requestAffiliatePayout; a required HEADER for standbyVirtualMachine, resumeVirtualMachine, updateVirtualMachineCloudInit, and deleteVirtualMachineCloudInit; an optional header for registerAccount outside the confirm map; and a required BODY field (idempotency_key) for createServiceAddon, upgradeService, submitVirtualMachineRenewal, changeVirtualMachineTerm, approveQuotation, submitPaymentCheckout, and submitCreditTopup.
x-mcp-argument-convention.read_vs_write_asymmetry: reverse-DNS: the READ (GET /vm/{service_id}/rdns) returns 'ptr'; the WRITE (POST) body field is 'hostname'. Use 'hostname' on write.

x-shc-zk-derivation.description: Normative OpenAPI extension fragment for SHC zero-knowledge backup recipient derivations. The formulas mirror the self-documenting style of the centurymetadata record preamble: every registered recovery value is either a native age recipient or a deterministic mapping from customer-held material to one age X25519 recipient. SHC stores public recipients and metadata only; customer secrets are never sent to SHC.

version.description: Fragment schema version for this derivation contract.

age_recipient_model.description: Common output model for every rung that produces an age X25519 recipient.

identity_formula.description: Secret recovery value emitted by shc-age.py when recovering a rung.
identity_formula.value: age_identity = uppercase(bech32(hrp='age-secret-key-', data=secret32)); checksum is computed over the lowercase hrp

public_fingerprint.description: Stable non-secret display fingerprint for a registered recipient.

rungs.description: Named recovery and recipient kinds supported by the SHC ZK backup registration model.

passphrase.description: Passphrase recovery rung. A customer passphrase and the service KDF salt derive the age X25519 secret.

registration_kind.description: Recipient kind written in the public registration block.

argon2id_alg.description: Password KDF algorithm used through libsodium crypto_pwhash.

argon2id_context.description: Domain-separation string prepended to the passphrase before KDF input assembly.

argon2id_password_input.description: Exact byte string passed as the password argument to libsodium crypto_pwhash.

argon2id_salt.description: Per-service 16-byte KDF salt from GET /vm/{serviceId}/data-preferences kdf.salt, encoded as 32 lowercase hex characters.

argon2id_output.value: seed32 = crypto_pwhash(32, ctx || NUL || passphrase, salt16, ops=3, mem=268435456, alg=argon2id13)

secret_derivation.description: Final age X25519 secret derivation from the KDF output.

recovery_card.description: Paper recovery-card rung. The 24 BIP-39 words are a raw 32-byte age secret with only the standard entropy checksum; there is no KDF.

registration_kind.description: Recipient kind written in the public registration block.

wordlist.description: Canonical BIP-39 English wordlist identified by sha256 of the newline-terminated list.

codec.description: Raw entropy codec used by shc-age.py.
codec.value: 24 words encode 256 bits of entropy plus the first 8 checksum bits of sha256(entropy32)

btc.description: Bitcoin-wallet recovery rung. SHC consumes BIP-85 derived entropy, never a bitcoin private key.

registration_kind.description: Recipient kind written in the public registration block.

bip85_path.description: Published SHC convention path for BIP-85 hex application output.

input_formats.description: Accepted encodings for the same 32-byte derived entropy.
input_formats.value: 64 lowercase or uppercase hex characters, or the equivalent 24-word BIP-39 raw-entropy encoding

nostr.description: Nostr software-key recovery rung. Remote-signer or hardware-only users should prefer the recovery-card rung because SHC needs the raw nsec to recover.

registration_kind.description: Recipient kind written in the public registration block.

input_formats.description: Accepted encodings for the customer-held nostr secret key.
input_formats.value: nsec1 bech32 or 64 hex characters, both decoding to 32 bytes

hkdf_salt.description: Salt passed to HKDF. shc-age.py uses RFC 5869 zero-length salt mode.

pgp.description: PGP self-wrap rung. SHC performs no derivation because PGP keys are often hardware-bound or non-exportable.

registration_kind.description: Recipient kind written in the public registration block.

derivation.description: Customer mints a fresh age identity and self-wraps it to their own PGP key outside SHC.

recovery.description: Customer decrypts their own PGP envelope to recover the age identity, then uses age -d.

ssh.description: Native age SSH recipient. This is a recipient kind, not an SHC secret derivation rung.

registration_kind.description: Recipient kind written in the public registration block or data-preferences recipient entry.

age_recipient.description: Native OpenSSH public key line consumed by age with -R.
age_recipient.value: ssh-ed25519 or other age-supported SSH public key line

derivation.value: none; age uses native SSH recipient support
